summary: mddriver fetches and executes remote code from jsonkeeper.com on import
details: The package advertises itself as a 'MongoDB connection driver' but ships no MongoDB code. index.js is a verbatim copy of Node's internal path module with an appended dropper: a base64-encoded URL (`aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanNvbmtlZXBlci5jb20vYi9DNEgwTQ==`, decoding to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/C4H0M) is decoded via atob, fetched, and the response's `data.content` field is passed to the Function constructor for execution. The dropper is invoked from a top-level IIFE (`(function(){ loadTokenData(); })();`), so any `require('mddriver')` immediately runs whatever JavaScript the attacker has posted to the jsonkeeper paste at that moment. jsonkeeper.com is a mutable, anonymous paste host — the executed payload can change at any time without a package republish. The misleading description plus the wholly unrelated path-module body act as a cover story for the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
